role_title: Technical Program Manager, Robotics Data Acquisition

level_signals: 5+ years of experience in technical program management, systems engineering, or operations
Owns and scales complex, cross-functional programs end-to-end
Leads roadmap and execution across multiple sites and stakeholder groups

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience in technical program management, systems engineering, or operations in hardware, robotics, or infrastructure-heavy environments
Strong systems-level thinking across hardware, software, and operational workflows
Proven ability to lead complex, cross-functional programs with high ambiguity
Experience scaling physical systems (labs, factories, robotics fleets, or test infrastructure)
Comfortable working close to hardware and debugging real-world system issues
Strong analytical skills; able to model capacity, throughput, and system performance
Excellent communication skills with the ability to align diverse stakeholders

scope.surface: Robotic data acquisition systems across development and evaluation environments (DAQ stations, sensors, compute, operator interfaces, data pipelines)
scope.business_model: internal R&D / AI model training infrastructure
primary_metrics_inferred: Data quality and integrity for model training
Throughput and capacity utilization of DAQ stations
SLA adherence for data collection workflows
core_responsibilities: Lead the roadmap, execution, and scaling of robotic DAQ systems, ensuring alignment with research, engineering, and operational priorities
Coordinate across robotics hardware, software, infrastructure, and operations teams to deliver tightly integrated, deployment-ready data collection systems
Translate experimental and research workflows into repeatable, scalable DAQ processes with clear SLAs, metrics, and reliability targets
Ensure DAQ stations (robots, sensors, compute, operator interfaces) are fully integrated, validated, and ready for production use across multiple sites
Build and manage detailed program plans, identify risks early, and drive mitigation across technical and operational domains
Model and plan DAQ capacity (stations, operators, uptime) to meet evolving data demands, balancing speed, cost, and quality
Partner with engineering and data teams to define and enforce data quality standards, ensuring consistency and usability for downstream model training
Drive improvements in system reliability, utilization, and efficiency through instrumentation, feedback loops, and process optimization

green_flags: Role owns a clearly defined product area (robotic DAQ systems) with concrete scope
Sits at intersection of Robotics Engineering, Operations, and Infrastructure — broad exposure
OpenAI Robotics team working toward AGI-level intelligence — high-impact mission
Clear list of core responsibilities with specificity (SLAs, capacity modeling, multi-site deployment)
red_flags: No location specified — city, remote eligibility, and visa sponsorship all unclear
No explicit nice-to-haves provided, making it harder to gauge relative competitiveness

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 5+ years requirement and scope of ownership)
scope.business_model (inferred from context: internal R&D/model training infrastructure)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated explicitly in JD)
location.type (inferred as onsite based on references to 'multiple sites', 'physical systems', 'DAQ stations'; no remote or hybrid language present)

ambiguities: Location/city is not specified anywhere in the JD. The reference to 'multiple sites' and physical hardware implies onsite but no city is named.

All requirements appear under 'You might thrive in this role if you:' which is softer language than 'Required' or 'Must have', but the items are substantive experience requirements. Treated as hard must-haves given the unconditional framing and 5+ year threshold.
